question.text: The index-based market-wide circuit breaker system in Indian stock exchanges applies at which stages of index movement?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** Circuit breakers apply at 10%, 15%, and 20% of index movement either way. When triggered, they halt trading in all equity and equity derivative markets nationwide. They are triggered by movement of either BSE Sensex or Nifty 50, whichever is breached first. **The Trap:** These are nationwide coordinated halts, not exchange-specific. Both BSE and NSE halt trading simultaneously. **Cross-Exam Context:** Circuit breakers are tested in X-A (Ch 6), VIII (Equity Derivatives), and VII. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Circuit breakers prevent panic-driven crashes by giving the market time to absorb information. Individual stock price bands (2%, 5%, 10%, 20%) are separate from index circuit breakers.
